title: Pears in red wine – let the evening's troubles disappear
excerpt: Pears in red wine is a light dessert. It's especially good after a rather rich and heavy meal. You feel less guilty about eating dessert and even feel like you're getting your dose of vitamins in. So cook those pears . 8 pears 1 bottle of red wine of your choice 250 ml sugar …

seo.title: Pears in red wine - let the evening's troubles disappear - Marinda Kook

html:
Pears in red wine is a light dessert. It's especially good after a rather rich and heavy meal. You feel less guilty about eating dessert and even feel like you're getting your dose of vitamins in. So cook those pears .

  • 8 pears
  • 1 bottle of red wine of your choice
  • 250 ml sugar or castor sugar
  • 2 star anise
  • 2 cloves
  • 2 sticks cinnamon.
  • peel of one lemon – grate on the finest side of your grater
  • 10 ml maizena
  • 60 ml water

NOW:

  1. put the red wine, sugar, cinnamon sticks, star anise, cloves and peel of one lemon in a pot on the stove.
  2. stir it over medium heat until the sugar has dissolved.
  3. peel the pears but keep the little stems on – it looks pretty
  4. put the pears in the wine mixture and cook for around 20 minutes – feel them, the pears should still be a little firm.
  5. take the pears out of the sauce and put them in your prettiest bowl
  6. mix the maizena in a cup with the water – it forms a paste
  7. now mix the maizena into the warm sauce on the stove, let it simmer for 5 minutes – this thickens your sauce
  8. pour the syrup over your pears and enjoy it with cream, custard or ice cream.

You can eat the pears cold or warm. Remove the star anise, cinnamon sticks and cloves before serving. You can make the pears the day before.
